title: PHI Lab Receives NIH R01 Grant for Clinical Provenance Research
description: We are thrilled to announce that our lab has been awarded an NIH R01 grant to develop provenance-aware frameworks for clinical AI systems over the next four years, totalling $2.1 M USD in direct costs.

title: Paper Accepted at JAMIA: Traceable AI Recommendations in Clinical Decision Support
description: Our work on provenance-aware clinical decision support has been accepted for publication in the Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association (JAMIA), one of the top venues in biomedical informatics.

title: Prof. Kim Delivers Keynote at AMIA 2025 Annual Symposium
description: Prof. Hyojung Kim will deliver a keynote lecture on data provenance and trustworthy AI at the AMIA 2025 Annual Symposium in San Francisco, CA.

title: We Are Hiring: Two PhD Positions Open for Fall 2026
description: PHI Lab is recruiting two fully-funded PhD students starting Fall 2026 in the areas of clinical NLP and federated learning for healthcare. Applicants with strong ML and programming backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

title: Welcome to Three New PhD Students Joining PHI Lab in Spring 2025
description: PHI Lab warmly welcomes three exceptional doctoral students who will be contributing to our clinical NLP, federated learning, and health data governance projects throughout 2025.

title: Best Paper Award at IEEE ICHI 2024 for EHR Phenotyping Work
description: Our paper on federated EHR phenotyping across institutional boundaries received the Best Paper Award at the IEEE International Conference on Healthcare Informatics (ICHI) 2024 in Orlando, FL.

title: New Preprint: Benchmarking LLMs on Korean and English Clinical NER
description: We posted a preprint benchmarking seven large language models on clinical named entity recognition tasks in both Korean and English EHRs, with results spanning 12 entity categories and three hospital datasets.

title: PHI Lab Hosts Annual Health AI Workshop at The Catholic University of Korea
description: Researchers and clinicians attended a one-day workshop on responsible AI in healthcare, featuring invited talks, poster sessions, and a panel discussion on regulatory challenges in clinical AI deployment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellowship Position Available — Data Provenance in Health AI
description: We are seeking a postdoctoral researcher to lead our NSERC-funded project on provenance engineering for clinical ML pipelines. The two-year fellowship includes a competitive salary and travel funding.

title: Paper on Knowledge Graph Clinical Decision Support Published in npj Digital Medicine
description: Our multi-year project on knowledge-graph-integrated clinical decision support has been published in npj Digital Medicine, reporting pilot study results from two partner hospitals showing a 14% improvement in guideline adherence.
